source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/DK_제품별 자료 정리(pdf)/4. A1417MCW/6. IEC Test Report (EMC, Safety)/PRJ0042257 REP028549 CBTR IEC60601-1 H&abyz A1717MCW (Original)_CSV.pdf

document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report REP028549
document.documentType: IEC 60601-1 safety test report chunk with attachments
document.revisionOrVersion: TRF No. IEC60601_1U; Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 revision 0
document.documentDate: 2023-11-07 for mapped Software validation report deliverables

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk covers IEC 60601-1 thermal, ingress, single fault, mechanical strength, flammability, IEC 62304 software, evidence mapping, equipment photos, and national deviation attachment facts for detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory wiki facts for safety test coverage, pass verdicts, model accessories, software lifecycle evidence, and national deviation scope in CB test report REP028549.
keyPoints: Excessive temperature testing under clause 11.1.1 was marked P across multiple supply configurations for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
Ingress/cleaning tests under clauses 11.6.5 and 11.6.6 were marked P; IP54 detector testing and 70% isopropyl alcohol cleaning showed no wetting of electrical insulation.
Single fault conditions under clause 13.2 were marked P, including short-circuit tests where equipment shut down immediately without hazardous situation.
Mechanical strength tests under clause 15.3 were marked P, including push, impact, drop, and mould stress relief tests.
Software attachment identifies IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 compliance evidence and classifies the software as safety class B.

entities.name: REP028549
entities.evidence: Pages 146-192 repeatedly identify Report No. REP028549.

entities.evidence: Pages 146-151 list Model No. A1717MCW in excessive temperature testing.

entities.name: A1417MCW
entities.evidence: Pages 152-158 list Model No. A1417MCW in excessive temperature testing.

entities.name: F1417MCW
entities.evidence: Pages 159-165 list Model No. F1417MCW in excessive temperature testing.

entities.evidence: Supplementary information states testing was conducted with power supply box, model DEPS9601A.

entities.evidence: Pages 150 and 157 state testing was conducted with AC/DC adapter, model BM090S15F.

concepts.name: Excessive temperatures in ME equipment
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.1.1 tables are titled Excessive temperatures in ME EQUIPMENT and verdict P.

concepts.name: IP54 ingress protection test
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.6.5 / Ingress of water or particulate matter lists IP54 Detector and no wetting of electrical insulation.

concepts.name: Cleaning validation with 70% isopropyl alcohol
concepts.evidence: Testing was performed with cleaning agent (70 % Isopropyl alcohol) recommended by the manufacturer.

concepts.name: Single fault condition safety
concepts.evidence: Clause 13.2 SINGLE FAULT CONDITIONS table has verdict P and hazardous situation entries marked No for short-circuit tests.

concepts.name: Mechanical strength tests
concepts.evidence: Clause 15.3 table lists Push, Impact, Drop, and Mould Stress Relief tests with Pass observed results.

concepts.name: Vertical burning V-2 classification
concepts.evidence: Additional test under IEC 60695-11-10 Method B states complies with V-2 classification.

concepts.name: IEC 62304 software safety class B
concepts.evidence: Software safety classification section states Classified as software class B.

concepts.name: Software lifecycle evidence mapping
concepts.evidence: Attachment maps required IEC 62304 evidence to Software validation report A-TCF-5-131, revision 0, date 2023-11-07.

concepts.name: National deviations attachment
concepts.evidence: National deviations list United States, Canada, Japan, and Korea.

evidence.claim: A1717MCW excessive temperature testing under IEC 60601-1 clause 11.1.1 passed across tested supply configurations in this chunk.
evidence.evidenceText: Pages 146-151 show 11.1.1 TABLE: Excessive temperatures in ME EQUIPMENT, Model No. A1717MCW, verdict P, including 90 V/60 Hz, 264 V/50 Hz, 264 V/60 Hz, AC/DC adapter, and 11.55 Vdc battery tests.

evidence.claim: A1417MCW excessive temperature testing under IEC 60601-1 clause 11.1.1 passed across tested supply configurations in this chunk.
evidence.evidenceText: Pages 152-158 show 11.1.1 TABLE: Excessive temperatures in ME EQUIPMENT, Model No. A1417MCW, verdict P, including power supply box, AC/DC adapter, and rechargeable Li-ion battery pack configurations.

evidence.claim: F1417MCW excessive temperature testing under IEC 60601-1 clause 11.1.1 passed across tested supply configurations in this chunk.
evidence.evidenceText: Pages 159-165 show 11.1.1 TABLE: Excessive temperatures in ME EQUIPMENT, Model No. F1417MCW, verdict P, including power supply box and 11.55 Vdc rechargeable Li-ion battery configurations.

evidence.claim: The temperature measurements were normalized by adding measured rise to a maximum allowed ambient temperature of 35 °C.
evidence.evidenceText: Supplementary notes state the measured temperature is determined by measuring temperature rise of the part under test and adding it to the maximum allowed ambient temperature (35 °C) specified in the technical description.

evidence.claim: Power configuration 1 used power supply box model DEPS9601A.
evidence.evidenceText: Supplementary information states: Testing was conducted with power supply box, model DEPS9601A (power configuration 1).

evidence.claim: Power configuration 2 used AC/DC adapter model BM090S15F.
evidence.evidenceText: Supplementary information states: Testing was conducted with AC/DC adapter, model BM090S15F (power configuration 2).

evidence.claim: The equipment is not intended for use in oxygen-rich environments.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.2.2.1 supplementary information states the equipment is not intended for use in oxygen rich environments; clause 13.2.11 repeats this for oxygen rich environments.

evidence.claim: Resistance method for winding temperature was not used.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.1.3d Temperature of windings by change-of-resistance method is N/A, with supplementary information: Resistance method is not used.

evidence.claim: Ingress and cleaning tests passed with no wetting of electrical insulation and subsequent dielectric strength and leakage current compliance.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.6.5 IP54 Detector and 11.6.6 Cleaning both state no sign of wetting on electrical insulation; supplementary note states equipment complied with dielectric strength and leakage current test after the test.

evidence.claim: Cleaning test used manufacturer-recommended 70% isopropyl alcohol.
evidence.evidenceText: Supplementary information states testing was performed with cleaning agent (70 % Isopropyl alcohol) recommended by the manufacturer.

evidence.claim: Fire enclosure was provided for the equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 13.1.2 supplementary information states: Fire enclosure is provided.

evidence.claim: Short-circuit single fault conditions did not create hazardous situations.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 13.2.2 reports EC455 short-circuited at 264 V~ for 10 min and rechargeable Li-ion battery output short-circuited at 11.55 Vdc for 10 min; both shut down immediately, with HAZARDOUS SITUATION marked No.

evidence.claim: No thermostats, temperature limiting devices, liquid, ventilation fans, ventilation openings, filters, cooling agent, moving parts, motors, or mechanical hazards were provided or identified for the listed single fault clauses.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 13.2 entries state no thermostats, no temperature limiting devices, no liquid, no ventilation fans/openings/filters/cooling agent, no moving parts, no motors, and no mechanical hazard.

evidence.claim: Mechanical strength testing passed for detector, switch box, and exposure switch enclosures.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.3 table lists Push Test 250 N, Impact Test 500 g steel ball from 1.3 m, Drop Test, and Mould Stress Relief 70 °C for 7 h with observed result Pass.

evidence.claim: Transformer short circuit and overload tests were evaluated through separately certified power supply box and AC/DC adapter reports.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 15.5.1.2 and 15.5.1.3 state evaluated in separately certified power supply box test report and AC/DC adapter test report, see attachments 5 and 6.

evidence.claim: The test report does not cover ME system leakage current requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 16.6.1 supplementary information states this test report does not cover requirements for ME systems, refer to general product information and other remarks.

evidence.claim: Vertical burning testing according to IEC 60695-11-10 Method B complied with V-2 classification.
evidence.evidenceText: SP table Additional or special tests conducted, clause 11.3, states vertical burning test according to IEC 60695-11-10 (Method B) and observed result complies with V-2 classification.

evidence.claim: The medical device software is classified as IEC 62304 software safety class B.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment Software IEC 62304:2006+AMD1:2015 clause 4.3 states Classified as software class B and marks the class B criterion P.

evidence.claim: No legacy software was considered in the IEC 62304 assessment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.4 LEGACY SOFTWARE states No legacy software considered, with related legacy software activities marked N/A.

evidence.claim: Software requirements include security, IT-network, regulatory, user interface, functional, input/output, interface, alarm/warning/message, data, installation, operation, maintenance, and user maintenance requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.2.2 lists software requirements categories including SECURITY requirements, IT-network aspects, regulatory requirements, user interface requirements, and other listed categories, with verdict P.

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software development, verification, testing, release, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution evidence is mapped to Software validation report A-TCF-5-131 revision 0 dated 2023-11-07.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment Software mapping lists deliverables from clauses 4.3, 5.1-5.8, 7.1-7.4, 8.1-8.2, and 9 with title Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131), revision 0, date 2023-11-07.

evidence.claim: Equipment photo attachments cover external and internal views of A1717MCW, A1417MCW, F1417MCW, power accessories, rechargeable battery, switch box, and exposure switch.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 1 picture pages list external views for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, F1417MCW; internal views; PCB views; power supply box DEPS9601A; AC/DC adapter BM090S15F; rechargeable Li-ion battery; switch box; and exposure switch.

evidence.claim: National deviations are included for the United States, Canada, Japan, and Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 National deviations lists United States (US), Canada (CA), Japan (JP), and Korea (KR).

evidence.claim: The US national differences attachment is based on AAMI ES60601-1:2005 and amendments AMD1:2012 and AMD2:2021.
evidence.evidenceText: US National Differences page states differences according to National standard AAMI ES60601-1:2005, ES60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012, ES60601-1:2005/AMD2:2021.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 4 of 13; document-level title, report issue date, certificate status, and full scope may appear in other chunks.
The date 2023-11-07 is explicitly tied to mapped Software validation report deliverables, not necessarily the overall IEC 60601-1 report date.
Some referenced attachments, such as separately certified power supply box and AC/DC adapter reports in attachments 5 and 6, are cited but not included in this chunk.
